body.title: Tools — self-registering, AI-agnostic tool registry (Igor)
body.summary: Tools are the action surface Igor's reasoners call. The registry is AI-agnostic: a tool knows nothing about which tier or provider calls it, and reasoner adapters convert the registry schema to whatever protocol the caller speaks (Anthropic tool-use vs OpenAI/OR). Each tool module self-registers at import time via registry.register(Tool(...)) — no manual wiring — and LOW-inertia modules are hot-reloadable, their registration re-executing on reload. A Tool carries name/description/parameters/fn and execute() never raises (it returns an error string on an unknown tool). A reactive habit can carry a code_ref + TTL so a habit fire dispatches a tool and pushes the result to TWM, giving Igor capabilities (e.g. current time) without an LLM call.

key_concepts.name: self-registration
key_concepts.what: each module calls registry.register(Tool(...)) on import; tools/__init__.py imports all modules so registration is automatic

key_concepts.name: AI-agnostic + schema normalization
key_concepts.what: to_anthropic_schema / to_openai_schema / to_text_description; adapters translate per caller, tools stay protocol-blind

key_concepts.name: execute() never raises
key_concepts.what: unknown-tool and error cases return a string, not an exception — the reasoner loop can't be crashed by a bad tool call

key_concepts.name: reactive habit pattern
key_concepts.what: a PROC habit with code_ref + twm_ttl_seconds dispatches a tool on fire and pushes the result to TWM with a self-cleaning TTL

body.status_notes: Live in the igor device. The source doc's '118-tool inventory' and capabilities_index.dsb are point-in-time and not carried forward; the tool roster drifts, the registry mechanism is the durable intent. Self-edit/hot-reload tools have their own intent-point ([[self-edit]]).
